content.text: Customer Communications Templates for GenAI Incidents

Customer communications during an AI incident carry higher stakes than the equivalent communication for a conventional service outage. When a payment service is down, the problem is unambiguous. When an AI assistant produces wrong or harmful outputs, customers have additional questions: was my data affected? Was the AI making recommendations I acted on? Should I trust it going forward? Customer comms templates for AI incidents must address these specific concerns while avoiding language that creates unnecessary legal exposure or magnifies distrust.
Decision Matrix: Who to Notify and When

attrs.alt: Customer communication decision matrix for AI incident types

content.text: Template Library by Incident Type

# Customer Email Templates — GenAI Incident Communications
# Usage: IC selects appropriate template, fills placeholders,
# submits for Legal review if required, then sends via Customer Success.

## TEMPLATE 1: AI Quality Degradation (Capability Failure, SEV-2)
Subject: Service Notice: {Product Name} AI Performance

We are writing to let you know that {Product Name}'s AI assistant
experienced degraded accuracy between {start_time} and {end_time} UTC
on {date}.

During this period, AI responses may have been less accurate than our
normal standards. Tasks completed during this window may benefit from
re-verification.

We have resolved the issue and normal AI performance has been restored.
A full analysis will be available in our postmortem at {postmortem_url}
within {N} business days.

We apologize for the disruption and remain committed to the reliability
you expect from us.

---

## TEMPLATE 2: Safety Policy Violation (SEV-1, Legal review required)
# IMPORTANT: Do NOT send without Legal approval.
Subject: Important Notice Regarding Your {Product Name} Account

We are writing to inform you of an incident affecting some interactions
with {Product Name}'s AI assistant.

Between {start_time} and {end_time} UTC on {date}, we identified that
some AI responses did not meet our content standards. We have taken
immediate steps to prevent recurrence and have suspended the feature
pending a full investigation.

If you received AI responses during this window that you found
concerning or that influenced important decisions, we encourage you to
review those outputs. Please contact our support team at {support_email}
if you need assistance reviewing any affected interactions.

We take the quality and safety of our AI systems extremely seriously
and will share a full postmortem at {postmortem_url} within 48 hours.

[Requires Legal review before sending. Do not modify this language.]

---

## TEMPLATE 3: Service Outage (Infrastructure, SEV-1)
Subject: Service Disruption — {Product Name} AI Features Unavailable

{Product Name}'s AI features were unavailable between {start_time} and
{end_time} UTC due to {brief non-technical description}.

During this period, requests to the AI assistant returned errors or
did not complete. No data was lost. Features have been fully restored.

We apologize for the interruption. A detailed postmortem will be
published at {postmortem_url} by {postmortem_eta}.

---

## TEMPLATE 4: Security Incident / Data Exposure (SEV-1, Legal MANDATORY)
# MANDATORY: Legal and DPO must approve. Regulatory notification required.
# Use only the language below. Do NOT add additional detail without Legal review.
Subject: Important Security Notice — Action May Be Required

We are writing to inform you of a security incident affecting
{Product Name}.

On {date}, we identified unauthorized access that may have affected
your data. We immediately contained the incident and have notified the
relevant authorities as required by applicable law.

Affected information may include: {list of data types — Legal to confirm}

Steps we are taking: {Legal-approved list of remediation steps}

Steps we recommend you take: {Legal-approved user guidance}

If you have questions, contact our dedicated response team at
{incident_email}.

We sincerely apologize for this incident and remain committed to
protecting your data.

[Legal and DPO MUST review and approve before sending.]
Review and Approval Workflow

Customer communications that require Legal review cannot be sent by the IC unilaterally. The approval workflow must be fast enough not to cause a regulatory deadline miss while thorough enough to prevent statements that create legal liability.
IC selects the appropriate template and fills all placeholders. Unclear placeholders (e.g., the exact scope of affected users) must be confirmed with the Technical Lead before the draft is forwarded.
Draft is posted in the incident Slack channel and shared with Customer Comms Lead, Legal (if required by the matrix), and the VP of Engineering.

Legal has a 30-minute SLA for review of safety templates and a 60-minute SLA for security/breach templates. If Legal cannot meet the SLA, they must say so immediately so an alternative reviewer can be found.
Approved draft is sent by Customer Success via the official email platform with required legal footers attached. IC confirms delivery and posts confirmation in the incident channel.
Language Anti-Patterns for AI Incidents

The language used in customer communications becomes evidence in regulatory investigations, customer dispute escalations, and litigation. Engineers who draft comms templates must understand what language creates unintended legal exposure.
Anti-Pattern
Why It Is Problematic
Better Approach
"The AI hallucinated"
Jargon that customers do not understand; may imply systemic unreliability
"Some AI responses may have been inaccurate"
"No data was breached" (before investigation is complete)
Premature assurance that creates liability if investigation reveals otherwise
"We are investigating whether any data was affected"
"This won't happen again"
Absolute promise that cannot be guaranteed; creates expectation liability
"We have taken steps to prevent a recurrence"
"Human error caused this"
Assigns individual blame; undermines blameless culture and may be defamatory
"Our investigation identified a process gap that has since been corrected"
Post-Resolution Follow-Up

Resolution is not the end of the customer communication obligation. For SEV-1 and SEV-2 incidents, a post-resolution follow-up within 5 business days demonstrates accountability and helps retain trust with enterprise accounts.
Share the postmortem summary—simplified for non-technical stakeholders—with affected enterprise accounts via a Customer Success call or written briefing.
For SEV-1 incidents, offer a service credit in accordance with the SLA terms. Proactively applying credits without waiting for the customer to request them is a meaningful trust signal.
For safety or security incidents affecting enterprise accounts, offer a 1:1 briefing with your CISO or Head of Trust and Safety. Enterprise procurement teams expect this level of accountability.
Enterprise Account Notification Automation

For large organizations with many enterprise accounts, manually identifying affected accounts and sending personalized notifications is impractical within the required SLA. Build automation that queries which accounts had active sessions during the incident window and segments them by impact severity for targeted outreach.
GDPR and Regulatory Notification Obligations

AI incidents that involve personal data processing failures create statutory notification obligations in regulated jurisdictions. These are not optional communications—they are legal requirements with penalties for non-compliance. The communications team must know these deadlines before any incident occurs.
Regulation
Trigger Condition
Notification Timeline
Recipient
GDPR (EU)
Personal data breach risking rights/freedoms
72 hours to regulator; without undue delay to data subjects if high risk
Supervisory Authority + affected individuals
CCPA (California)
Unauthorized access to nonencrypted PI
Expedient; before AG if 500+ residents
California AG + affected residents
HIPAA (US healthcare)
Breach of unsecured PHI
60 days (HHS); without unreasonable delay to individuals
HHS OCR + affected individuals
EU AI Act (2025+)
Serious incident from high-risk AI system
15 days (serious); 2 days (life/health risk)
Market surveillance authority

The GDPR 72-hour clock starts when the organization becomes aware of the breach—not when the investigation is complete. If you suspect a breach, notify the DPO immediately and start the regulatory notification process even before root cause confirmation. You can update the notification as new information is gathered. Missing the 72-hour window is a separate violation from the breach itself.
Key Takeaways
AI incidents require templated comms that address AI-specific concerns—accuracy doubts, action-based harm, data trust—that conventional outage templates do not cover.
Legal review is mandatory before sending any safety or security incident communication. The 30/60-minute SLA on Legal review must be established before any incident occurs.
Language anti-patterns—premature assurances, absolute promises, jargon, and individual blame—create legal exposure. Template language must be pre-approved by Legal and used verbatim.
Post-resolution follow-up with affected enterprise accounts is expected at the SEV-1 and SEV-2 level. Proactive SLA credit application demonstrates genuine accountability.
